title: Ethanol ecosystem: Judging fear by noise is not evidence; is an index the answer? 15 stocks from the ecosystem
summary: Sugar is one of the oldest distressed sectors in India. For decades it has swung between glut and shortage, carried heavy debt, was dependent on politically-set cane prices, and left farmers unpaid. Many of the companies have been financially weak for years, some for most of their listed lives. Ethanol was meant to change all that, giving mills a second, steadier stream of income and changing the whole ecosystem. Has it? Check out Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, for price targets of over 4,000 listed stocks along with detailed company analysis focusing on five key components – earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum – to generate standardised scores. SR+ Reports is a complimentary offering to ETPrime members.

title: Businesses that inch up even as the market consolidates: 5 mid- and small-cap stocks with an upside potential of up to 26%
summary: This is a market to be selective in, not brave in. The safer way to play a turn in the market is to stay with businesses whose demand does not depend on the mood on the street, the state of the economy, or the direction of the Nifty.

title: Reporting errors, not evasion, spark most tax litigations, say experts
summary: The most avoidable source of tax litigation between individual taxpayers and the department is disputes over classification and disclosure—specifically, mismatches between how taxpayers report income or investments and how the department interprets them.

title: Home loan EMI: Why paying more than 30% of income could hurt your financial freedom
summary: A high home loan EMI can strain monthly finances and delay wealth creation. Experts recommend keeping EMIs within 25-30% of monthly income to maintain savings, investments and financial security while managing homeownership comfortably and preparing for unexpected expenses.

title: FPIs reverse 4-month selling trend with Rs 15,157 cr inflow in Jul
summary: In July, foreign investors turned net buyers of Indian equities following a period of large outflows, prompted by favorable domestic macroeconomic trends and improved global risk sentiment. The Indian debt market also saw a significant influx of foreign investments, attributed to recently modified debt taxation that enhanced its attractiveness. The continuity of these inflows is contingent upon both global developments and the pace of India's economic growth.

title: Dmart recasts strategy as rapid delivery competition intensifies
summary: DMart has scaled back its e-commerce footprint, exiting underperforming cities to focus on metros as competition intensifies in India’s rapid delivery space. Despite rising pressure from Amazon, Flipkart, Zepto and Swiggy, the retailer is sticking to its low-cost model and non-10-minute delivery approach amid muted earnings performance.

title: Late-stage cheques go big at $86 million average this year
summary: Late stage funding in India saw average deal sizes surge to about $86 million in the first half, driven by fewer but larger transactions. Capital flowed into AI infrastructure, energy and lending, while investors turned selective, focusing on profitability, governance and exit visibility amid a more disciplined private market environment.

title: India's beverage market is moving beyond fizzy drinks. FMCG companies are racing to catch up

title: EPF Scheme 2026: Lost your job? Full PF withdrawal now allowed only after 12 months
summary: The new EPF Scheme, 2026 replaces the decades-old 1952 scheme and changes how provident fund withdrawals work after unemployment. Members can now access only 75% of their PF balance immediately after losing a job.

title: More mutual funds don’t always mean better diversification: How to avoid the portfolio overlap trap
summary: According to the expert, investors should add a new mutual fund only if it reduces portfolio concentration, adds meaningful diversification, and fits your financial goals. A compact portfolio of 5 to 6 funds can often provide better diversification.&nbsp;

title: ₹39.96 lakh BSNL VRS payout gets full tax exemption from ITAT; here's what the ruling means
